title: CS598 Bench — per-instance descriptor
description: One file per vulnerability case at instances/<project>.<id>/instance.json. Mirrors the build/trigger fields from projects/<project>/vulns/<id>.yaml so a benchmark loader can consume a single self-contained directory without re-reading the full vuln yaml. Repository paths are NOT recorded explicitly — by convention, sanitizer build lives at <bench_root>/../repos-sanitizer/<project_name>/ and coverage build at <bench_root>/../repos-llvm-cov/<project_name>/.

project_name.description: Matches a folder under projects/ AND under repos-sanitizer/ / repos-llvm-cov/.

lang.description: Primary language of the affected source file (drives some build/harness defaults).

base_commit.description: SHA of the *vulnerable* commit to checkout & build. Default-derived as fix_commit^; mirrors the vuln yaml's `vulnerable_commit` field. Loaders should checkout this exact SHA in both sanitizer and coverage clones before running build_sh_*.

build_sh_sanitizer.description: Shell snippet that produces an ASAN/UBSAN-instrumented binary at build_target. Run from the sanitizer clone root with ASAN_OPTIONS=use_sigaltstack=0 set. Multi-line strings are fine. Should be local-only (no full TF/PyTorch wheel) where possible.

build_sh_cov.description: Same shape, produces an llvm-cov instrumented binary at build_target. Run from the coverage clone root.

build_target.description: Path to the artifact (binary or shared lib) RELATIVE to the clone root, after build_sh_* completes (e.g. 'build/bin/whisper-cli', 'build/lib/libopencv_wechat_qrcode.so').

harness_command.description:
CLI template the agent runs to feed a testcase to build_target. MUST contain the literal placeholder '<path/to/testcase>' which a runner substitutes with the absolute path of the PoC input. Examples:
  '${REPO}/build/bin/whisper-cli -m ${MODEL} -f <path/to/testcase>'
  'python -c "import cv2; cv2.imread(\"<path/to/testcase>\")"'
Use ${REPO} as a placeholder for the repo root if needed; the runner expands it to the appropriate clone path.

vuln_location.description: '<file>:<line>' relative to the project repo root. Used by DrillAgent mode 2 (vuln_input_mode='vuln_location') as the pivot for VAA/PEA.

expected_fingerprint.description: Multi-line free-text holding the canonical sanitizer fingerprint to compare against on validation runs. Substring matching (case-sensitive) on key frames + the ASAN error type + nearby source lines. Empty if validation_status != validated.

poc_input_path.description: Path RELATIVE to instances/<id>/repro/ pointing at the canonical PoC blob/script that reproduces this vuln (e.g. 'poc_advisory.py', 'overflow_poc.gguf'). Empty string allowed for no-PoC cases (validation_status='invalidated_no_poc').

triggerable_class.description: Drives the runner's execution strategy. See vuln.schema.yaml for definitions.

validation_status.description: Ground-truth label set after running the curated PoC against the built env. See vuln.schema.yaml::validation_status for the four meanings.

validation_notes.description: Free-form notes set by scripts/validate_instance.sh: failure mode, environment requirement, partial repro, last-attempted timestamp, etc.
